summary: Created the Batch G admin data ingestion architecture artifact as a constrained brownfield extension of the existing student indicator load flow. The architecture keeps the feature inside the current admin surface, limits approved business writes to measurements and checkpoints, adds preview/apply orchestration, backup-before-apply, execution audit, and operator-assisted rollback.

decisions.title: Scope boundary
decisions.detail: Batch G is limited to initial indicator ingestion for one selected student enrollment at a time; it does not create a generic repository-wide JSON importer.

decisions.title: Frontend placement
decisions.detail: The feature lives inside the existing /app/admin surface as a dedicated panel keyed by panel=ingestao-dados, reusing AdminShell and current student context.

decisions.title: Backend contract
decisions.detail: Preview and apply are split into explicit endpoints under the existing admin_students route namespace, while the old apply endpoint remains a compatibility bridge during migration.

decisions.title: Persistence and duplication
decisions.detail: Approved business targets are measurements and checkpoints only, with duplication frozen to replace_enrollment to preserve current repository semantics.

decisions.title: Operational safeguards
decisions.detail: Apply requires a successful preview execution, creates a snapshot before writing, persists execution audit records, and triggers restore on post-snapshot failure.

decisions.title: Source handling
decisions.detail: The architecture records source metadata now but activates only manual_assisted in the MVP; json_file is reserved for a later approved expansion.

risks.impact: Stakeholders may expect a broader multi-entity ingestion feature because the brief is wider than the current brownfield seed.
risks.mitigation: The artifact explicitly narrows Batch G to indicator ingestion and flags broader entity ingestion as deferred work requiring a new architecture decision.

risks.impact: The existing snapshot utility captures the registered storage set, which may be heavier than a store-scoped backup.
risks.mitigation: Reuse the existing proven snapshot flow for safety in MVP, and revisit store-scoped backups only in a future approved optimization.

risks.impact: Keeping the current apply endpoint as a bridge can create temporary dual-path confusion if not documented.
risks.mitigation: Route the legacy endpoint through the new orchestration path and document the deprecation/migration sequence in implementation stories.

risks.impact: If the operator needs structured file ingestion immediately, the MVP architecture will not satisfy that operational need yet.
risks.mitigation: Treat json_file as the first follow-on expansion after Batch G approval rather than widening this stabilization batch.
